questions.question: What is the role of a chiller vs a cooling tower in a water-cooled HVAC system?
questions.userAnswer: A chiller chills water, a cooling tower expels heat from the condenser water loop using ambient air and evaporation.
questions.feedback: Perfect. Chiller cools the chilled water loop, cooling tower rejects condenser heat via evaporation and ambient air.

questions.question: What is the difference between EER and SEER, and which matters more for commercial/industrial systems?
questions.userAnswer: No answer.
questions.feedback: EER measures efficiency at a fixed condition (95°F, full load). SEER measures across a full season. EER is more relevant for commercial/industrial — those systems run sustained loads, not seasonal cycles.

questions.question: Define gross margin for a service business and what it tells you about operations.
questions.userAnswer: Revenue minus cost of sales = gross profit. Shows operational efficiency before G&A and selling expenses. Forgot depreciation.
questions.feedback: Correct. For HS, cost of sales = direct labor, materials, subcontractors. Depreciation is the expense type you blanked on. Gross margin shows how efficiently you're delivering the service before overhead.

questions.question: If revenue is $500,000 and COGS is $320,000, what is gross profit and gross margin %?
questions.userAnswer: Gross margin $180,000 and 36%.
questions.feedback: Clean math. $500k - $320k = $180k gross profit, $180k / $500k = 36%.

questions.question: Name all 5 divisions of Hunton Services and briefly describe each.
questions.userAnswer: Commercial Services, Industrial Services, BAS, Special Projects, HRS. Described each correctly.
questions.feedback: All 5 correct with accurate descriptions.

questions.question: What is billable backlog, how does it affect cash flow, and what does a shrinking billable backlog indicate?
questions.userAnswer: Complete but not yet invoiced work. Shrinking is good for cash flow. Initially confused with pipeline backlog — distinction clarified in follow-up.
questions.feedback: Correct after clarification. Billable backlog = complete, not invoiced. Shrinking = faster invoicing = better cash flow. Key HS note: WIP and billable backlog are separate reports. WIP clears when job completes, billable backlog clears when invoiced.

sessions.note: Strong first quiz. 92% after correction. Sharp on operations and financial math. HVAC efficiency metrics need work.

questions.question: What is compressor surge on a centrifugal chiller and what causes it?
questions.userAnswer: No answer.
questions.feedback: Surge = flow reversal when pressure ratio exceeds what the impeller can handle. Sounds like a whomp/bang. Causes: low load, low refrigerant flow, fouled condenser. Trane chillers will unload or trip on surge.

questions.question: What is a VFD and how is it used on commercial HVAC equipment?
questions.userAnswer: No answer.
questions.feedback: Variable Frequency Drive — controls motor speed by varying electrical frequency. Allows partial load operation vs on/off cycling. Major energy saver. Trane uses VFDs heavily on CenTraVac centrifugal chillers, pumps, and AHU fans.

questions.question: Revenue $1.2M, COGS $840k, Operating expenses $180k. Calculate gross margin and operating margin.
questions.userAnswer: GM $360k (30%), Operating income $180k (15%). Said 15% is better.
questions.feedback: Math correct. Framing note: whether 30% GM and 15% operating margin are 'good' depends on historical trend and benchmarks. The right read: 30% shows operational efficiency, 15% shows overhead is managed. Context always matters.

questions.question: What is WIP and why is it important for Hunton Services teams?
questions.userAnswer: Work in progress — active jobs being worked. Critical for monthly financial forecasting at HS. Each team forecasts WIP monthly.
questions.feedback: Excellent. Added nuance: stuck WIP (90% complete, not finishing) distorts forecasts. Worth watching.

questions.question: Describe the team structure within a Hunton Services commercial team.
questions.userAnswer: ASM (P&L owner, team manager) > RC (admin/dispatch) / RCA (RC assistant) > Field Supervisor (job walks, safety, actual work, supervises techs) > Technicians.
questions.feedback: Perfect. Every role accurate. Good catch on FS reporting nuance.

questions.question: Describe HRS's business model and the two main rental lines.
questions.userAnswer: Rents chillers and generators to commercial/industrial. Two lines: planned rentals (redundancy during maintenance) and emergency rentals. Adding generator service division.
questions.feedback: Accurate. Key talking point: HRS has the 2nd largest 500-ton chiller fleet in the nation — a real competitive moat.

weaknesses: HVAC technical knowledge (starting from zero — expected)
sessions.note: 58% — pulled down by zero HVAC technical background. All operational and financial answers were strong. Quiz rules updated: HVAC questions now start from ground zero, Trane-specific models, broad to specific progression.

questions.question: What are the main components of an AHU?
questions.userAnswer: Outside damper, coils, fan wall, ductwork.
questions.feedback: Partially correct. Key miss: filters (most important maintenance item). Full list: coils, fan/blower, filter section, dampers (outside air + return air), mixing box. Ductwork is downstream distribution, not part of the AHU.

questions.question: Difference between air-cooled and water-cooled chillers — when to use each?
questions.userAnswer: Air-cooled uses fans + ambient air. Water-cooled uses cooling towers, higher tonnage, more precise — best for hospitals and critical applications.
questions.feedback: Excellent. Added note: water-cooled more energy efficient at full load — key data center selling point for HT.

questions.question: Revenue $1.2M, COGS $660k, OpEx $120k — calculate GP, GP%, OP, OP%. What is ASM responsible for?
questions.userAnswer: GP $540k (45%), OP $420k (35%). ASM responsible for gross margin only — that's what they control.
questions.feedback: Perfect math and accountability. ASMs own direct costs which drive gross margin. G&A overhead above is outside their control.

questions.question: Biggest cost risk in a service business when work slows down?
questions.userAnswer: Labor — techs leave if no work. Fewer hours = less burden absorbed = hurts overhead offset.
questions.feedback: Strong. Burden absorption point is sophisticated — more hours = more burden absorbed = overhead offset. Correct.

questions.question: Explain DTB, DBO, and Pay Velocity. What is the DTB goal at HS?
questions.userAnswer: DTB = days from job complete to invoice. DBO = days from invoice to payment. Pay Velocity = full chain. DTB goal = 5 days. High billable backlog is a symptom of high DTB, not the cause.
questions.feedback: Best answer of any quiz session so far. Perfect chain, correct goals, and the symptom vs. cause distinction on billable backlog is real operational understanding.

questions.question: When would a building rent a 500-ton chiller from HRS vs buying?
questions.userAnswer: Planned maintenance on chiller with no redundancy, one of two chillers down, no budget for new equipment, or prefer off-balance-sheet as an operating expense.
questions.feedback: Perfect. All scenarios covered. Off-balance-sheet preference is CFO-level thinking.

sessions.note: 92% — strong session. Best single answer was the DTB/Pay Velocity explanation. AHU filters are the one gap to watch.

questions.question: What is the role of a cooling tower in a water-cooled chiller system?
questions.userAnswer: Rejects heat from condenser loop. Chiller can't reject this heat itself so cooling tower is needed.
questions.feedback: Perfect. Clean and accurate.

questions.question: What is a VFD and how does it save energy on commercial HVAC equipment?
questions.userAnswer: Allows motors to run at variable speed rather than full on/off. Running at 70% instead of 100% saves energy.
questions.feedback: Solid. Add: energy savings are non-linear (cube law) — 50% speed uses ~12.5% of energy, not 50%. That's why VFDs are so impactful on large equipment.

questions.question: Financial calculation question (full question not captured in grading session).
questions.userAnswer: 30% GM and $700k.
questions.feedback: Partial credit — answer appears correct but full math not shown. Always show revenue, COGS, and calculation. Partial answers get partial credit.

questions.question: What does utilization measure and why is it important at Hunton Services?
questions.userAnswer: Measures how much techs are being used. Shows if over/understaffed. When maxed = time to hire. Also measures ASM and RC's ability to keep techs dispatched strategically.
questions.feedback: Excellent and nuanced. Utilization is both a staffing metric AND a management execution metric. Both dimensions captured correctly.

questions.question: What is DTB and why does it matter?
questions.userAnswer: Completion to invoice date. Tracks cash flow and financial health.
questions.feedback: Correct but thin — you've answered this with much more depth before. Full answer includes: 5-day goal, feeds into DBO, both part of Pay Velocity, high DTB = cash flow drag. Don't leave detail on the table.

questions.question: Which Hunton Services divisions are most predictable and why?
questions.userAnswer: Commercial Services and BAS. Both have transactional + large job mix for steadiness, and contract customers for reliable recurring revenue. BAS can have rough patches being more project-dependent.
questions.feedback: Strong business analysis. Both characteristics correctly identified: transaction/large job mix + contract revenue floor. Accurate note on BAS rough patches.

weaknesses: Show full financial math — partial answers get partial credit
DTB answers going thin when you know it well
sessions.note: 85% — solid session. HVAC fundamentals clicking. Financial math question was ambiguous without full context. DTB answer was correct but below your own standard. Show your work.

questions.question: 12-story building, floors 4-7 warmer than 8-12, chiller running fine. Most likely culprit?
questions.userAnswer: B — AHU serving floors 4-7 has fouled coil or failing fan
questions.feedback: Correct. Chiller is fine, cold water available — problem is distribution. AHU issue is the right isolation.

questions.question: Calculate EBITDA and EBITDA margin. Revenue $4.2M, Op income $756k, Depreciation $126k, Interest $42k.
questions.feedback: Close but wrong. EBITDA = $756k + $126k + $42k = $924k (22.0%). You answered $882k which is EBIT (adds back depreciation only, not interest). EBITDA adds back ALL four: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, Amortization.

questions.question: $900k contract, 55% complete, $820k total cost estimate. Revenue to recognize and margin concern?
questions.userAnswer: B — $495k recognized, margin concern at ~8.9% gross margin flagged now
questions.feedback: Perfect. Percentage-of-completion = 55% × $900k = $495k. $820k cost on $900k contract = 8.9% margin, needs to be flagged immediately, not at closeout.

strengths: Revenue recognition and WIP margin analysis
weaknesses: EBITDA vs EBIT distinction — interest expense must be added back
sessions.note: 67% — first multiple choice quiz format. Q1 and Q3 strong. Q2 missed EBITDA vs EBIT distinction by one step. New format working well — faster to answer.

questions.question: 25,000 sq ft flat-roof office building — which system does Hunton recommend?
questions.userAnswer: B — Rooftop packaged unit (RTU)
questions.feedback: Correct. RTUs are the dominant choice for mid-size commercial flat-roof buildings. Self-contained, roof-mounted, right-sized. Chillers are for much larger buildings (100K+ sq ft). Core Hunton Services work.

strengths: Commercial HVAC system selection — RTU vs chiller scale distinction
sessions.note: 100% — 1 question format working well. Clean answer, no hesitation. RTU knowledge is solid.
